page: Microsoft Azure B4ms is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the BS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 4 vCPUs and 4 physical cores operating at 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and is equipped with 33 GB of local storage. This instance does not include a GPU and is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate that the B4ms exhibits weak single-core and multi-core CPU performance, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench tests. While cached memory bandwidth is average at 359.39 GB/sec, uncached read and write memory bandwidths are weak. Passmark database operations and memory scores perform in the average tier, whereas Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput fall into the bottom 25%. LLM inference speeds for a small 135M model are average, but drop significantly for larger models.

The burstable CPU allocation model offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads with variable CPU utilization, allowing users to save on resource costs during idle periods. However, the tradeoff is limited sustained CPU performance, as evidenced by the weak benchmark scores under continuous load. This server is suitable for low-traffic web serving, small database operations, development or test environments, and light background tasks that do not require continuous high CPU throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure B4ms is a burstable virtual machine from the BS family, utilizing an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 4 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it includes 33 GB of local storage and lacks a GPU. Benchmark data shows weak single-core and multi-core CPU performance, alongside weak uncached memory bandwidth, though cached memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier. Because of its burstable CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads with variable CPU demands. It is best suited for development environments, small databases, and low-traffic web servers that experience intermittent usage spikes rather than sustained heavy computational loads.

tagline: A burstable x86_64 virtual machine with balanced memory and local storage for workloads with variable CPU utilization.
